name: Individual member enrolled successfully
description: Documented POST success response (Individual Member Enrollment output) showing the created person account contactId, loyalty program member id, and the two created transaction journals (Member Enrollment accrual + Member Referral accrual). Mirrors the JSON example at lines 29728-29755 with realistic Salesforce ids.
notes: Path uses ${loyaltyProgramName} placeholder exactly as documented (resource line 21019). Version shown as /services/data/vXX.X in the doc's Example (line 21023) - left literal in sfPath. Available version 55.0; note referredByMemberReferralCode is 57.0+ and linkNumber-style partner fields are unrelated. Request body Root XML tag is <individualMemberEnrollment>. Request properties: additionalMemberFieldValues (Required, Map<String,String>), associatedContactDetails (Required, Loyalty Contact Input), enrollmentDate (Required, Date YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss in org timezone NOT UTC), memberStatus (Required: Active/Inactive/Custom), membershipNumber (Required); optional: canReceivePromotions, canReceivePartnerPromotions, createTransactionJournals, enrollmentChannel (Pos/Web/Email/CallCenter/Social/Mobile/Store/Franchise/Partner/Print), membershipEndDate, referredBy, referredByMemberReferralCode (57.0), relatedCorporateMembershipNumber, transactionJournalStatementFrequency (Monthly/Quarterly), transactionJournalStatementMethod (Mail/Email). The doc lists request property table values "true"/"false" as quoted strings in the JSON example - kept as documented. Doc shows NO error/empty response example for this resource, so only one success fixture built. Documented ids were short (e.g. 0lMxx00000008FJ); I extended them to full 18-char Salesforce style per task instructions. transactionJournals item shape comes from the inline response JSON example (Transaction Journal Output[] - no separate property table grep hit found at line 29783). Open question: whether 200 or 201 is the real status code (doc does not state HTTP status); assumed 200.
